title: Smoky Crisp Vietnamese Phyllo Dough Bao
description: A delicious and crispy Vietnamese-inspired pastry filled with a savory beef and vegetable mixture, finished with a rich and smoky glaze.

ingredients.name: Beef
ingredients.notes: Lean beef can be used for a lower-calorie option.

instructions: Layer 4-6 sheets of phyllo dough in a large bowl, brushing each layer with a little bit of vegetable oil.
In a large pan, cook the beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the onion, garlic, mushroom, and cabbage to the pan and cook until the vegetables are softened.
Add the soy sauce, sambal oelek, brown sugar, salt, and pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Transfer the beef and vegetable mixture to a bowl and stir in the fish sauce.
Brush the edges of the phyllo dough with a little bit of water and place a spoonful of the beef and vegetable mixture onto the center of each sheet of phyllo dough.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal the bao.
Place the bao on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with the smoky glaze.
Bake the bao in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
Let the bao cool for a few minutes before serving.
